steps.title: Use two points on the fit line
steps.content: The line of best fit passes near $(12,480)$ and $(24,880)$. These are approximate readings from the drawn line, not exact data points from the scatterplot.

steps.title: Estimate the slope
steps.content: Use the change in sales divided by the change in temperature: $$m\approx\frac{880-480}{24-12}=\frac{400}{12}\approx33.$$ So a reasonable equation should have slope about $33$.

steps.title: Find the intercept
steps.content: Use the point $(12,480)$ with $d=33t+b$: $$480=33(12)+b$$ $$480=396+b$$ $$b=84$$ Thus an equation of the line of best fit could be $d=33t+84$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D is $d=33t+84$, so choice D is correct. Choice C has the right slope but an intercept that is much too high, and choices A and B have slopes that are too small.
